stories.title: Macron praises Europe’s predictability in face of countries that ‘hurt you without even informing you’ – Europe live
stories.summary: In an apparent swipe at US, France’s president says that with some countries you do not know ‘whether tomorrow they won’t… hurt you’ Meanwhile, the European Union has sought to ramp up pressure on Hungary to drop its veto on the €90bn loan for Ukraine, with the European Commission saying it will push ahead with its preparatory work for the loan to be paid out. The commission said it would draft a legal text setting out the details of the first payment of €45bn in 2026 and what the funds would be

stories.title: Wednesday briefing: ​How did Russia become the unexpected beneficiary of the Iran war?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As conflict engulfs Iran, shifting ​global alliances and soaring energy prices are reshaping ​the existing power balances​ that could redefine the next stage of international security Good morning. So far, there is only one clear winner from the war in Iran: Russia. Before the US and Israel attacked Tehran in late February, Moscow was preparing deep budget cuts to education and healthcare funding to pay for its invasion of Ukraine, which has now entered its fifth year. In

stories.title: Brussels unveils emergency change to EU carbon market to prevent soaring prices
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission has proposed amending its cap-and-trade system to boost the supply of pollution permits and shield industry from soaring carbon costs, in the first of a string of changes to the EU’s most important climate policy. The EU’s Emissions Trading System, which requires companies to pay for their pollution by purchasing permits, has come under increasing pressure from heavy industry in recent months, who say it is adding extra expense on top of already sky-high energy

stories.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
stories.summary: Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A US government panel on Tuesday exempted oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act (ESA), a move which critics say could doom a rare whale species and harm other marine life . stories.title: Red shoes protest in Romania highlights femicide crisis
stories.summary: Red shoes at Bucharest's Romanian Athenaeum honour women killed by domestic violence, as Romania adopts its first femicide law and tougher penalties.

stories.title: Clearing up the scandal that has cast suspicion on the Hungarian secret service is entirely up to the government
stories.summary: It has been days since Direkt36 published an article about an unusual police and intelligence operation, after which an investigator came forward and claimed that his unit had been pressured by the Hungarian intelligence services to conduct house searches at the homes of two IT specialists linked to the Tisza Party. There are no signs of the scandal abating any time soon, and the information which has come to light so far has become highly politicized. One of the IT specialists involved in the c

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Viktor Orbán has the support of both Russia and the US. Here’s why that doesn’t seem to be doing him any good | Péter Krekó
stories.summary: In Hungary, the Iran war is exposing tensions. On April 12, voters may decide that Orban’s geopolitical contortions are a liability On 3 March, Viktor Orbán held a phone conversation with Vladimir Putin. According to official Hungarian reporting, the discussion focused on “energy issues” and other routine matters. What followed was anything but routine. Within days, the Hungarian foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó , had flown to Moscow, and returned with two freed prisoners of war , dual citizens

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Ukraine calls Hungary ‘a disgrace’ after leaked calls with Moscow emerge
stories.summary: Kyiv urges inquiry after leaked calls appeared to capture Hungarian foreign minister telling Moscow he would try to amend EU sanctions to its liking. What we know on day 1,498 Continue reading...

stories.summary: Budapesten és Sajószentpéteren is gázolt a vonat.

stories.summary: A Direkt36 birtokába jutott dokumentumok szerint a külügyminiszterhez közel álló Benkő Szilárd cége dolgozott a CATL-gyár egyik részén. A kormányon belül Szijjártó Péter volt a gyárépítés egyik lelkes támogatója.

stories.summary: A Boston Consulting Group által készített tervezetet a kormány a fiókban hagyta.

stories.title: Közel egymilliárd forintért szervezte meg Balásy Gyula cége a budapesti CPAC-et
stories.summary: A rendezvény, aminek főszónoka Orbán Viktor volt, azonban még ennél is drágább lehetett.

stories.title: Megjött a bátorságuk: Lázár emberei büszkén pózoltak Orbán ócsai fórumán a Telexnek
stories.summary: Lázár János vasárnap megtapsoltatta az országjárás közönségével a szervezett skandálókat, akik kedden egy csoportképre is összeálltak a Telexnek. A miniszteri biztos és a tartalékos katona mellett már Lázár többi embere is bátran mosolyog.

stories.title: Arte: Hiába olcsóbb Magyarországon a benzin, a helyi pénztárca így is nehezen bírja
stories.summary: És hiába a magyar ársapka: egy ilyen húzás sokba kerülhet az államnak.

stories.title: Minden nap eljövök ide a templomba, és imádkozok a Tisza győzelméért
stories.summary: Magyar Péter kedden hat fórumot is tartott, a sztártémák: Szabó Bence nyomozó, Gundalf, Szijjártó Péter. Békésen karszalagokat dobáltak a színpadról, Gyulára harmadszorra tért vissza.

stories.title: Ezekért az előnyökért tényleg érdemes új építésű lakást vásárolni! (x)
stories.summary: Mitől lesz egy új építésű lakás valóban jó választás – az elhelyezkedéstől, a műszaki tartalomtól vagy attól, hogy milyen életet lehet benne élni? A hamarosan költözhető, Duna-parti Waterside Residence esetében mindhárom tényező kiemelkedő. Megnéztük, mit kínál ma az egyik legizgalmasabb új budapesti lakópark-fejlesztés.

stories.title: Magyar tervezésű hajlítható játékkonzol érdekel? (x)
stories.summary: Még mielőtt mindenki rohanna a boltokba, nézzünk a naptárra, és mondjuk ki magunkban: április elseje. De azért játsszunk el egy kicsit a gondolattal, hogy miért ne robbanthatná be egy új magyar találmány a játékkonzolok piacát? Hiszen megannyi világhírű találmány kötődik magyar tudósokhoz a biztonsági gyufától, a töltőtollon keresztül a telefonközpontig. Jó szórakozást a következő néhány percre, és ajánljuk további böngészésre a cikk végén elhelyezett részletes tájékoztató oldalát is!

stories.title: Angliában szívbetegségek megelőzésére is ajánlják a népszerű súlycsökkentő szereket
stories.summary: Azoknak a betegeknek, akiknek a BMI-je 27 felett van, és akiknél magas a szív- és érrendszeri betegségek kockázata.

stories.title: Az oroszbarát tyituski módszer: keménykötésű figurákat küldtek rá az utcán az ellenzékre
stories.summary: Orbán országjárásán is megjelentek azok a kormányközeli kötődésű embereknek, akiknek az ellenzéki hangok elnyomása a feladata. A jelenség emlékeztet arra, ahogy a Janukovics-rezsim emberei, a tyituskik léptek fel az ellenzékkel szemben Ukrajnában, de nálunk nem történt olyan erőszak, ami az oroszbarát rendszerekben általános volt.

stories.summary: Az orosz külügyi szóvivő örült annak, hogy közzétették a felvételt.

stories.title: Árulónak nevezett Pintér Sándor? Nem biztos, hogy tudja, az állomány mit gondol – Szabó Bence az elmúlt napokról
stories.summary: Miért lepte meg, hogy hogyan fogadták a rendőrségen? Mit gondol arról, hogy Orbán baleknak nevezte? Interjú a Tisza elleni műveletről borító Szabó Bencével.

stories.title: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg shopping area in Germany's first attack in decades
stories.summary: Wolves are now present in almost all EU countries, and their numbers have increased from 11,000 in 2012 to more than 20,000 in 2023. Italy, Bulgaria, Romania and Spain have populations of more than 2,000.

stories.title: In an Istanbul market, I found an old German phrase book – and a reminder of how not to speak to migrants | Carolin Würfel
stories.summary: Turkish immigrants to Germany in the 60s were seen as temporary labour, not people. Today’s government in Berlin is at risk of repeating the mistake A few weekends ago, I went to the flea market in Bomonti, a neighbourhood on the European side of Istanbul. I go there regularly, and over the years I’ve accumulated a small collection of things: embroidered napkins, records, old issues of House & Garden, earrings, candle holders. It is usually on the days when you are not looking for anything i

stories.title: Cambodia: Alleged cyber scam boss extradited to China
stories.summary: Authorities say Li Xiong was arrested in Cambodia and deported at the request of Chinese authorities. He is linked to an accused scam center kingpin who was extradited in January.

stories.title: Three Reichsbürger backers face trial in Germany over coup and kidnapping plot
stories.summary: Three alleged backers of Germany's Reichsbürger "United Patriots" group face trial in Munich over supporting coup plans and a plot to kidnap ex-health minister.

stories.title: Trump says he’s considering pulling out of NATO
stories.summary: President Donald Trump rattled NATO allies on Wednesday morning, as he strongly suggested that the U.S. was weighing whether to pull out of the transatlantic military alliance. “I would say [it’s] beyond reconsideration,” Trump told the Telegraph newspaper , when asked if he would think twice about the U.S.’s membership of the alliance after the Middle East conflict is over. NATO allies have infuriated Trump by uniformly resisting joining the U.S. and Israeli assault on Iran, and rebuffed

stories.title: ‘Volatile’ world requires Britain to get close to EU, Starmer says
stories.summary: LONDON — The “volatile” international situation requires Britain to build a closer relationship with the European Union, Keir Starmer has said. Addressing reporters at Downing Street on Wednesday the U.K. prime minister said London and Brussels will flesh out plans for “closer economic cooperation” and tighter security ties at an upcoming summit. “It is increasingly clear that as the world continues down this volatile path, our long-term national interest requires closer partnership with our all

stories.title: Spain hits back at Trump in escalating row over euthanasia
stories.summary: Spain blasted the U.S. government on Wednesday for probing a high-profile euthanasia case in Barcelona, kicking off a diplomatic rift over human rights and end-of-life laws. The dispute was spurred by a leaked U.S. diplomatic cable ordering the American Embassy in Madrid to open an investigation into Spain’s decision to allow sexual assault victim Noelia Castillo to undergo legal euthanasia last week . After suffering repeated sexual assaults and surviving a suicide attempt that left her paralyz

stories.title: Prominent UK pro-Palestine activists guilty of breaching protest conditions
stories.summary: Campaigners call verdict on Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham ‘grotesque’ and part of attempt to ‘undermine civil liberties’ Two prominent leaders in the Palestine solidarity movement in Britain have been found guilty of breaching protest conditions, in what campaigners called a “grotesque” and “shocking” decision. Ben Jamal, 62, the director of the Palestine Solidarity Campaign (PSC), and Chris Nineham, 63, vice-chair of the Stop the War Coalition, were accused of failing to comply with conditions im

stories.title: Oil price falls and markets rally after Trump says Iran war over in ‘two to three weeks’
stories.summary: US president’s claim that conflict is nearing end prompts 15% drop in Brent crude and stock market climb in Asia Business live – latest updates Oil prices tumbled and stock markets have rallied across the world after Donald Trump said the war in Iran would end in “two to three weeks”. Brent crude, an international benchmark for oil, fell as low as $98.35 a barrel on Wednesday, down more than 15% on the previous day and its lowest level in a week. It later recovered some ground, down 2.5% on the

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump claims Iran’s president has asked US for a ceasefire
stories.summary: US president makes claim on social media ahead of his address to the nation later today ‘Get your own oil’: Trump launches tirade against Europe for not joining Iran war Houthi forces in Yemen have claimed responsibility for a missile attack on southern Israel this morning, saying it was a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah. In a statement, the Houthi movement said it carried out its third missile attack in the conflict “in conjunction with Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon”. Continue reading..

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill seven in Beirut as it vows to occupy southern Lebanon after war ends
stories.summary: Heavy strikes hit Lebanon's capital Beirut overnight as Israel claimed it had struck a "senior Hezbollah commander" and another member of the group. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said his country's military intends to occupy a swathe of southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Police disperse Jerusalem protest over Israel’s death penalty law
stories.summary: Israeli police broke up a protest outside parliament in Jerusalem as demonstrators voiced anger over a new law allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks.

stories.title: Iran strikes tanker off Qatar coast as Tehran's attacks on Gulf states persist
stories.summary: Qatar said it had intercepted two Iranian cruise missiles, while a third struck a fuel tanker off its coast. All 21 crew members were safely evacuated, with no injuries or environmental damage reported.

stories.title: Asia ramps up use of dirty fuels to cover energy shortfall triggered by Iran war
stories.summary: South Korea will delay the shutdown of coal-fired plants, while the Philippines also plans to boost the output of its coal-burning plants Governments across Asia are ramping up their use of coal, the dirtiest fossil fuel, as they try to cover huge energy shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran. The move has triggered warnings from climate experts who point to coal’s devastating environmental impact, and say the energy crisis should be a wake up call for governments to invest in renewab

stories.summary: Rightwing parties are using parliamentary queries, legal traps and policing to target NGOs and stifle dissent Revealed: Five EU governments found to ‘consistently’ dismantle rule of law Pauline Voss, the deputy editor of Nius, a fast-growing rightwing media outlet whose ambition is to be Germany’s Fox News, believes progressive civil society groups in Germany are engaged in a coordinated campaign to “act against their own population”. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says US will be 'leaving soon' as strikes hit Beirut, Tehran and central Israel
stories.summary: An Iranian missile has wounded 14 people in central Israel, while Israel bombed Beirut, killing seven. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has said the US could end its campaign in the next two to three weeks. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: New rules to tamp down gasoline price hikes
stories.summary: German gas stations will now only be able to raise pump prices once a day as costs spiral for motorists. Meanwhile, an umbrella group for German cities says skilled Syrian workers should be welcome to stay. DW has more.

stories.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: The plane crashed in a mountainous forest area not far from Crimea's capital, Simferopol. Since the Kremlin sent troops into Ukraine, incidents involving Russian military planes have occurred frequently.

stories.summary: Tensions on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border are escalating amid reports that Islamabad is trying to take over parts of Afghan territory.

stories.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.title: UK to host Strait of Hormuz summit this week
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain will host a virtual meeting of 35 nations Thursday in a bid to ease the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Speaking in Downing Street British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Wednesday his Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will bring together the nations behind last month’s joint statement urging safe passage through the waterway after Iranian forces de facto closed the vital shipping route. Starmer said the meeting “will assess all viable diplomatic and political measures that

stories.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
stories.summary: Lewis was the father of Avi Lewis, who was elected leader of the progressive New Democratic party one day before his father died Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer. Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88. Continue reading...

stories.title: Molly the border collie rescued after a week waiting for injured owner in New Zealand’s remote backcountry
stories.summary: A rescue mission involving volunteer helicopter crew and public donations ended in joy after Molly was located and brought home A spot of furry black and white appears among the jagged rocks of New Zealand’s alpine backcountry. It is Molly the border collie, sitting near the foot of a waterfall where she had been separated from her owner one week earlier. Molly was rescued on Tuesday after an avalanche of donations from the public funded a volunteer team made up of former helicopter pilots and c

stories.title: Finnish up! Claire Aho’s colour revolution – in pictures
stories.summary: The pioneering Nordic artist brought wit, verve and cinematic flair to postwar photography. A new exhibition celebrates her vibrant visual style Continue reading...

stories.title: Delayed by EU entry/exit system? Then travel light
stories.summary: Only way to avoid missing a flight because of EES rules: squeeze everything into a cabin bag and skip luggage check-in Travellers to the EU risk missing their flights because bag drop-off times don’t allow for the long queues to get through a new security system. My family of four missed our easyJet flight home from Málaga because, although we followed advice from the airport and arrived three hours before departure, the bag drop-off didn’t open until two hours before. Continue reading...

stories.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Iraq, US officials say
stories.summary: State department says it is working to ensure release of freelancer ‘as soon as possible’ after abduction in Baghdad Middle East crisis – live updates An American journalist, Shelly Kittleson, has been kidnapped in Baghdad by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, the US has said, as regional security deteriorates after the US-Israeli attack on Iran. Kittleson is a longtime freelancer in the region, reporting extensively from Syria, Iraq and Lebanon. Continue reading...

stories.title: US tech firm Oracle cuts thousands of jobs as it steps up AI spending
stories.summary: Company chaired by Trump ally Larry Ellison seeks to reassure investors that bet on AI infrastructure will pay off Oracle is cutting thousands of jobs as the US technology company seeks to reassure investors that its bet on AI infrastructure will pay off. The $420bn firm, headquartered in Austin, Texas, started making employees redundant on Tuesday, with thousands of Oracle’s 160,000-strong workforce expected to leave. Continue reading...

Plus, a rocket heads to the moon for the first time since 1972 Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. Donald Trump has launched a tirade against European countries that refused to join the Israel-US war against Iran, calling out the UK and France, as transatlantic relations continue their downward spiral and the war wreaks havoc on the global economy. stories.title: Man charged with firearms offences after Dover counter-terror police arrest
stories.summary: Khalid Ahmed, 24, from Ealing in west London, remanded in custody until hearing at Old Bailey A 24-year-old man accused of trying to smuggle 10 guns and ammunition into the UK as he drove off a ferry into Dover has been remanded in custody. Khalid Ahmed, from Ealing in west London, who is a dual Dutch and Irish national, appeared at Westminster magistrates court on Wednesday, after being charged overnight with firearms offences. Continue reading...

stories.title: American Heart Association releases dietary guidance counter to some Maha guidelines
stories.summary: Leading US heart health group recommends prioritizing plant-based protein over meat relative to US government The American Heart Association’s new nutrition guidance, released on Tuesday, emphasized a dietary pattern rich in vegetables, ​fruits and whole grains, prioritizing plant-based protein over meat. It also suggested limiting the ‌use of sugar, salt and ultra-processed foods and replacing full fat dairy with non-fat and low-fat dairy. Continue reading...

stories.title: Boy, 17, among new arrests over suspected arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances
stories.summary: Three people arrested on suspicion of conspiracy to commit arson, say police investigating Golders Green incident Counter-terrorism police have arrested two men and a boy in connection with a suspected arson attack last week on ambulances run by a Jewish charity in Golders Green, north London. The three people – a 19-year-old man, a 20-year-old man and a 17-year-old boy – were all arrested early on Wednesday, the Metropolitan police said. Continue reading...

stories.title: MP rejects Palantir’s claims that criticism of NHS England deal is ‘ideologically motivated’
stories.summary: Head of committee says it was appropriate for government to seek guidance on way out of £330m deal with US data company Claims by Palantir that concerns over the US data analytics company’s multimillion-pound NHS contract are “ideologically motivated” have been rejected by the chair of a parliamentary committee. It was also appropriate for the government to seek guidance on activating a break contract in the deal, said Chi Onwurah, a Labour MP who heads the science, innovation and technology sel

stories.title: US jibes at Royal Navy are uncomfortable because they have substance | Jamie Grierson
stories.summary: While Pete Hegseth has mocked the ‘big, bad Royal Navy’, the First Sea Lord has sounded the alarm about its readiness Middle East crisis – live updates The US defence secretary, Pete Hegseth, had his tongue firmly in his cheek when he singled out the “big bad Royal Navy” in a recent press update on the US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Hegseth’s sarcastic comment was only the latest in a long line of jibes against the capabilities and readiness of the British Royal Navy. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘On a whole other level’: rapid snow melt-off in American west stuns scientists
stories.summary: Experts say brutal March heat has left critical snowpack at record-low levels – and key basins in uncharted territory Snow surveys taking place across the American west this week are offering a grim prognosis, after a historically warm winter and searing March temperatures left the critical snowpack at record-low levels across the region. Experts warned that even as the heat begins to subside, the stunning pace of melt-off over the past month has left key basins in uncharted territory for the dr

stories.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
stories.summary: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role o

stories.title: Merz criticised after calling for Syrians in Germany to ‘go back’ home
stories.summary: The German chancellor has drawn condemnation from NGOs and members of his own government Friedrich Merz has drawn condemnation from NGOs and members of his own government after he called for the vast majority of Syrians living in Germany to “go back to their homeland”. The German chancellor, who was elected last year after promising a tough line on immigration in a bid to beat the far right, made the remarks during a visit to Berlin on Monday by the interim Syrian president Ahmed al-Sharaa . Con

stories.title: UK to pay France extra £16m in stopgap deal to patrol Channel beaches
stories.summary: Two-month arrangement aimed at preventing small-boat crossings comes as existing deal expires The UK will pay France an extra £16.2m to keep police patrolling Channel beaches and prevent a surge in small-boat crossings after negotiators failed to agree a permanent deal before a midnight deadline. The stopgap arrangement, which will last for two months, comes after French negotiators refused to agree to UK demands for further interventions and patrols to stop asylum seekers from reaching the UK v

stories.title: ‘Get your own oil’: Trump launches tirade against Europe for not joining Iran war
stories.summary: Many countries in Europe have called the conflict illegal, with some blocking Israeli and US planes from moving weapons through their airspace Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has launched a tirade against European countries that refused to join his war against Iran, calling out the UK and France, as transatlantic relations soured from the spiralling conflict that has wreaked havoc on the global economy. On his Truth Social website, the US president told governments worried about f

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

stories.title: India news: Jet fuel prices reach record high amid Iran war
stories.summary: The Indian government has stepped in to limit the impact of record-high jet fuel prices on airlines and air fares. Commercial LPG cylinders have also gotten more expensive. Follow DW.

stories.title: Trump's 2026 World Cup won't be a tournament for fans
stories.summary: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is being overshadowed by politics, and there is widespread criticism of US President Donald Trump. Germany fans are torn on whether to travel to the US.

stories.title: Migrant boat capsizes in Turkey leaving 18 dead while 21 were rescued
stories.summary: Turkish authorities say the vessel attempted to evade its Coast Guard before sinking. At least 18 people drowned in the incident, 21 were rescued and an unknown number were missing.

stories.title: Germany's Merz sees backlash over Syrian refugee comment
stories.summary: According to Chancellor Friedrich Merz, 80% of Syrians living in Germany are to return to their home country within the next three years. The chancellor's remark drew fierce criticism, followed by backpedaling.

stories.title: Iran war: Strikes on steel deal painful blow to economy
stories.summary: The US-Israeli strikes on Iran's two biggest steel mills will likely harm Tehran's ability to wage war, but could also cripple Iran's economy in irreversible ways.

stories.title: Undiagnosed TB pose challenge for South Africa, Mozambique
stories.summary: Africa is making progress in reducing tuberculosis (TB). But experts say missed cases and drug-resistant infections are complicating the fight, particularly in South Africa and Mozambique.

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives call cannabis legalization 'a nonstarter' as 2-year report is filed
stories.summary: Conservatives see a two-year report on Germany's partial legalization of cannabis as evidence that it's been a failure. While the findings may be more nuanced, researchers did identify several problems.

stories.title: Local elections in Serbia: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba, the 1st since US blockade
stories.summary: The tanker carrying some 730,000 barrels of crude oil is sanctioned by the US, the EU and the UK amid the war in Ukraine. It comes as Cuba struggles with severe shortages after Trump's total oil blockade since January.

stories.title: Court orders Trump to halt White House ballroom construction
stories.summary: The judge said US presidents are not owners of the White House and Congress must approve any changes to the building.

stories.title: US judge blocks Trump order that barred funding for NPR, PBS
stories.summary: A federal judge has blocked a presidential directive that barred funding for NPR and PBS media entities. Separately, an appeals court panel paused a federal judge's order to bring VOA employees back to work.

stories.title: 'My six-year-old has nosebleeds': Chiang Mai air pollution sparks health fears
stories.summary: It's peak fire season in the Thai city, which now ranks as one of the world's most polluted.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war could end in 'two weeks', with or without deal
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the war in Iran could end in 'two weeks, maybe three', adding that 'whether we have a deal or not, it's irrelevant.' The White House announced Trump will address the nation with an update on Wednesday night.

stories.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
stories.summary: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

stories.title: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
stories.summary: The rapper "started feeling very ill" mid-way through the show in New York, her representative told US media.

stories.title: Lorry drivers protest near Paris over soaring fuel prices
stories.summary: Dozens of lorries and tractors staged a slow-moving protest east of Paris to draw attention to rising fuel costs linked to the Middle East war.

stories.title: 'It's phenomenal!': Excitement in Florida ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: BBC’s Leyla Khodabakhshi speaks to locals and visitors in Florida ahead of the Artemis II mission blast off.

stories.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
stories.summary: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

stories.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.title: EU prosecutors seek to lift immunity of 11 Greek MPs in farm subsidy scam
stories.summary: European prosecutors have urged Greece to lift immunity for 11 MPs over a multimillion-euro farm subsidy fraud, piling pressure on PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.summary: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Traditional Easter ‘monas’ get a contemporary makeover in Barcelona
stories.summary: A chocolatier in Barcelona is reimagining traditional Catalan Easter cakes, known as “monas,” with bold colours, modern designs and less sugary flavours.

stories.title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
stories.summary: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

stories.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
stories.summary: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

stories.title: 'One leg in uniform, one in a suit': How one young German is helping reshape Europe's security
stories.summary: Léocadie Reimers represents a generation rethinking security, combining her experiences from military service with her work in private sector defence.

stories.title: Securing Iran's highly-enriched uranium stockpiles could prove risky, experts say
stories.summary: Experts, analysts and former US government officials say Washington's alleged plans to deploy ground units to Iran to retrieve uranium stockpiles could expose personnel to potential attacks, radiation and chemical risks.

stories.title: Surveillance video shows kidnapping of US journalist in Baghdad
stories.summary: Surveillance footage appears to show the abduction of US journalist Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad, as officials say she remains missing and an investigation into the kidnapping is ongoing.

stories.title: DR Congo declares national holiday after reaching World Cup for first time in 52 years
stories.summary: This is just the second time that DR Congo have reached the World Cup finals, sparking celebrations in Kinshasa.

stories.title: In California primary, an electoral disaster is looming for Democrats
stories.summary: Two months before a crucial primary in one of the most liberal states in the US, Democrats are facing a self-made political catastrophe. Too many candidates for governor are fragmenting the party's electorate and could make a Republican the successor of Gavin Newsom.

stories.title: Moment US strike sends massive fireball into sky over Iran’s Isfahan
stories.summary: Large explosions and fires were seen over Isfahan, a central Iranian city hosting military and nuclear facilities.

stories.title: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during north Jerusalem raid
stories.summary: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during an overnight raid in Kafr Aqab north of Jerusalem

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba after US allows passage despite energy blockade

stories.title: Indonesia arrests Scottish man sought by Spain in connection with international crime syndicate
stories.summary: Police in Scotland and Spain carried out raids in connection with Lyons' case last week resulting in multiple arrests with the help of Europol, the European Union law enforcement cooperation hub.

stories.title: US Army pilots who flew near Kid Rock's home suspended and then reinstated within hours
stories.summary: The crews of two military helicopters were suspended by the army, then told there would be "no investigation" by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.

stories.title: Iran war risks private credit crisis and AI bubble bursting, Bank of England warns
stories.summary: LONDON — The Bank of England warned Wednesday that the ongoing U.S. conflict with Iran could cause already-existing weaknesses in the financial system to bubble over, heightening fears around the risks that private credit and artificial intelligence stocks pose to stability. Policymakers at the Bank identified weaknesses in private credit markets, highly priced U.S. tech stocks, and hedge fund activity in the sovereign bond market as key concerns that had lingered in the financial system before

stories.title: Netanyahu says Iran war goals achieved 'beyond halfway point' as strikes continue across Middle East
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, Trump's partner in attacking Iran, said more than half of his military aims had been achieved, but both leaders refused to put a timeline on an operation that has ignited a month-long regional war and jolted global markets.

stories.title: Emergency services respond to fire in central Israel after Iranian missile attack
stories.summary: Emergency teams responded to a fire in central Israel after Iranian missile strikes, with several people reported injured.

stories.title: Japan allows divorced couples to negotiate joint custody of children for first time
stories.summary: The new rules are the first major change to the country’s laws governing child-rearing in more than a century Divorced couples in Japan will be able to negotiate joint custody of their children from Wednesday, in the first major change to the country’s laws governing child-rearing in more than a century. Previously, Japan’s Civil Code required couples to decide which parent would take custody of their children when they divorce . Continue reading...

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: US Supreme Court rejects Colorado's 'conversion therapy' ban
stories.summary: Colorado had banned therapy seeking to change the sexual orientation or gender identity of LGBTQ patients under 18. Over 20 US states and much of Europe, including Germany, have banned the practice.

stories.title: Senegal: President signs off on LGBTQ law doubling maximum jail term for same-sex relations
stories.summary: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye enacted the new law after it resoundingly cleared parliament earlier this month. It also penalizes those "promoting" or funding same-sex relations, as well as false allegations.

stories.title: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
stories.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

stories.title: With Spielberg's help, a 101-year-old Auschwitz survivor becomes warrior against hate
stories.summary: People who watch the countless interviews Ginette Kolinka gives cannot say that they didn’t know about the extermination of 6 million European Jews by the Nazis and their collaborators.

stories.title: Around 30% of France's teachers strike in protest against job cuts and low wages
stories.summary: The strike action comes as anger grows among France's teachers about job cuts, class closures and stagnating wages.

stories.title: This German village relies on renewables to avoid rising energy costs

stories.title: Police bust 'maze-like' drug tunnel to Morocco in Spanish exclave of Ceuta

stories.title: Slovenian women keep centuries-old ‘drsanke’ Easter egg art alive

stories.title: Syrian president discusses Iran war with British PM on first visit to London since fall of al-Assad
stories.summary: Ahmed al-Sharaa has managed to build relations with Western governments and made several overseas trips, including to the United States, France and Germany.

stories.title: Fire at chemical plant in Russia’s Nizhnekamsk leaves three dead

stories.title: Iran has 'necessary will' to end war but seeking guarantees, Pezeshkian tells EU

stories.title: Palm Sunday bird sellers in Mexico City cling to fading tradition

stories.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad and security forces pursuing captors, Iraqi officials say
stories.summary: It was not immediately clear if the kidnapping was related to the ongoing war, but Iran-backed militias in Iraq have launched regular attacks on US facilities in the country.

stories.title: Strikes hit southern Lebanese village, damage buildings and vehicles
stories.summary: Israeli strikes hit a village in southern Lebanon, damaging buildings and emergency vehicles as rescue teams responded

stories.title: Italian Christmas meal tragedy turns into murder inquiry
stories.summary: Tests results suggest the two women may have been killed with ricin, according to Italian media, but police still do not have a suspect.

stories.title: King's state visit to US to go ahead in April, but no Harry meeting
stories.summary: Despite political tensions between the US and UK, the King will travel to Washington next month.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.summary: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: Peacekeepers killed by roadside explosion in Lebanon, initial report finds
stories.summary: The deaths of the two Indonesian nationals came less than a day after another UN peacekeeper - also Indonesian - was killed in Lebanon.

stories.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
stories.summary: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.summary: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: Tiger Woods to 'step away and seek treatment' after crash
stories.summary: The golf champion was arrested and charged with driving under the influence after clipping a truck and rolling his car in Florida on Friday.

stories.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
stories.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was a "steward" of the White House, not the owner.

stories.title: 'Moonshots' and merch - what it's like to be on Florida's Space Coast ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: Huge crowds are gathering around the Kennedy Space Centre to see the launch of the historic moon mission.

stories.title: Billion-plus people, three million officials, 33 questions - India begins huge census
stories.summary: The two-phase exercise - the world’s most ambitious - will see three million officials count every Indian.

stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök azt mondta, az USA 2–3 héten belül lezárja az iráni konfliktust. Hírfolyamunk a közel-keleti háborúról.

stories.summary: Szerdára virradó éjszaka három vonatgázolás történt az országban.

stories.summary: Nyilvánosságra hozták, milyen eredményekre számítanak az egyes választókerületekben, Hadházy Ákost várják győztesnek Zuglóban.

stories.title: 4059 milliárd hiányzott a költségvetésből 2025-ben
stories.summary: A KSH legfrissebb adatai szerint egy kicsit kevesebb volt ezzel a GDP-arányos hiány, mint egy évvel korábban.

stories.summary: 2025-ben a gyanúsított olyan erővel ütötte meg a másik férfit, hogy az a földre zuhant, eszméletét vesztette, majd röviddel utána meghalt.

stories.summary: 20 hónapig tarthatnak a munkálatok, de Zelenszkij korábban azt mondta, áprilisban már működhet a vezeték.

stories.title: Közel 2,5 milliárdot vehet ki Mészáros Lőrinc az Opusból
stories.summary: Részvényenként 15 forintot fizet osztalékként az Opus, Mészárosnak pedig több mint 160 millió van a birtokában.

stories.summary: Without a unified response from Europe, some governments have announced measures to cap spiking prices.

stories.title: A flat white-tól a narancsborig: a KÜNST egész napra kinyit (x)

Márciustól nem kell megvárni a délutáni nyitást, hiszen hétfőtől szombatig már reggel nyolctól beülhetünk, ugyanis megnyílt a specialty coffee részlegük! Az esti műszakhoz hasonlóan kedves, elhivatott személyzettel találkozunk, ezért – és a hely adottságai miatt is – a környék otthon dolgozó lakóinak ajándék ez a reggeli nyitás.

Mi áll a feltétel

stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök erről azután beszélt, hogy külügyminisztere közölte, fel kell tenni a kérdést, hogy „miért is vagyunk a NATO-ban”.

stories.title: A Szondi utca alkotója poénból újragondolná a Nerdőt

stories.summary: Hajdú 2020-ban kapott felfüggesztett börtönt költségvetési csalás miatt.

stories.title: Csütörtökre rendkívüli közgyűlést hívott össze a győri polgármester a Fidesz kampányrendezvényén történtek miatt
stories.summary: Pintér Bence felkérdezné a rendőrséget, ehhez azonban közgyűlési határozatra van szükség.

stories.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
stories.summary: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
stories.summary: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools m

stories.title: Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
stories.summary: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

stories.title: Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead
stories.summary: Finnish authorities are investigating two drones that crashed in southern Finland. Kyiv apologized for the incident, with Ukraine's Zelenskyy saying he and Finland's Stubb are on the same page.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.summary: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
stories.summary: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

stories.title: Peace talks with Iran ongoing and progressing well, White House says
stories.summary: The comments come on the same day that President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran's oil export hub of Kharg Island and other oil wells and power plants if it does not agree to a deal to end the war soon.

stories.title: Israel approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
stories.summary: The bill would make the death penalty the default punishment for Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank found guilty of intentionally carrying out deadly attacks deemed "acts of terrorism" by an Israeli military court.

stories.title: Iran launches missile and drone barrage on Dubai, Kuwaiti tanker hit
stories.summary: At least five explosions were reported in Dubai on Tuesday morning as residents fled to shelters, Euronews journalists reported. The UAE authorities said they were "actively engaging" with the threat.

stories.title: Watch: EU governments are throwing billions at the petrol shock. Will it help?
stories.summary: European drivers face soaring fuel prices as the Iran war hits oil markets, prompting EU states to cut fuel taxes, cap prices and tighten regulation.

stories.title: Lebanese justice minister calls for Israel negotiations to avert humanitarian catastrophe
stories.summary: Lebanese justice minister warns of 'dramatic' situation in the country as Israeli floats extended buffer zone on its southern border. Nassar calls for diplomacy to avert a catastrophe as he lashes at Hezbolahh for sabotaging diplomatic efforts by not laying down weapons.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.summary: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
stories.summary: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

stories.title: Spheres of influence: the Bauhaus’s radical female photographers – in pictures
stories.summary: The images are famous, but the women who took them are often forgotten. An inspiring exhibition focuses on the pioneering ‘new vision’ of Marianne Brandt, Lucia Moholy and more Continue reading...

stories.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
stories.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

stories.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again
stories.summary: Having barely budged the previous night, the whale stranded off Germany's Baltic coast for a week finally moved again late Monday. But after showing signs of deterioration, experts aren't sure where it's going next.

stories.title: How to fix Germany's costly health care system
stories.summary: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

stories.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
stories.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

stories.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
stories.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

stories.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
stories.summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for much longer. Whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

stories.title: Germany and Syria 'working jointly' on refugee returns, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
stories.summary: On his first trip to Germany since ousting his country's longtime strongman Bashar al-Assad in late 2024, Syria's interim leader pledged to work with Germany to enable more Syrians to return.

stories.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
stories.summary: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.

stories.title: Inside the cockpit of RAF tanker during defensive mission against Iranian drones
stories.summary: Defence correspondent Jonathan Beale flies onboard a Royal Air Force Voyager as it refuels jets in the Middle East.

stories.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
stories.summary: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

stories.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
stories.summary: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.
